title: Transport conductors
rationale: The role of a transport conductor is heavily reliant on physical presence, real-time human interaction, and safety-critical monitoring in a dynamic environment. While ticketing and information dissemination can be automated, the core responsibilities of passenger assistance, emergency response, and physical safety checks remain difficult for AI to replicate.

title: Travel attendants and travel stewards
rationale: The role of a travel attendant is primarily focused on physical presence, safety monitoring, and interpersonal service in a dynamic, real-world environment. While AI may assist with administrative tasks like scheduling or inventory management, the core responsibilities of emergency response, passenger care, and physical service cannot be automated by current or near-future AI.

title: Travel consultants and clerks
rationale: The core tasks of this occupation, such as itinerary planning, reservation management, and information dissemination, are highly digital and information-centric. AI-driven platforms and chatbots are already capable of automating booking processes and providing personalized travel recommendations, significantly reducing the need for human intermediaries in these functions.

title: Travel guides
rationale: The role of a travel guide is fundamentally rooted in physical presence, real-time interpersonal interaction, and the ability to manage groups in dynamic, unpredictable environments. While AI can assist with information retrieval and itinerary planning, it cannot replace the human element of guiding, safety monitoring, and providing a personalized, engaging experience on-site.

title: Tree and shrub crop growers
rationale: Tree and shrub crop growing is primarily a physical, outdoor occupation that requires manual labor and real-time interaction with biological systems in unpredictable environments. While AI can assist with administrative tasks, market analysis, and precision agriculture monitoring, the core tasks of planting, pruning, and harvesting remain largely dependent on physical presence and manual skill.

title: Typists and word processing operators
rationale: The core tasks of this occupation, such as transcribing audio, typing from drafts, and editing text, are now fully automatable by advanced speech-to-text and large language models. Because the work is entirely digital and routine in nature, AI systems can perform these functions with higher speed and accuracy than human operators.

title: Undertakers and embalmers
rationale: The core tasks of this occupation involve physical handling of human remains, manual reconstruction, and sensitive, in-person interpersonal interaction with grieving families. While administrative tasks like scheduling or obituary writing may be assisted by AI, the physical and emotional requirements of the role provide a significant barrier to automation.

title: Underwater divers
rationale: Underwater diving is a highly physical, manual, and hazardous occupation that requires real-time human presence in unpredictable, non-digital environments. While remote-operated vehicles (ROVs) may assist with some inspection tasks, the core physical labor and complex decision-making required for underwater construction, salvage, and rescue remain fundamentally human-centric.

title: University and higher education teachers
rationale: University teaching involves significant knowledge work, such as curriculum design, research, and grading, which are increasingly susceptible to AI-assisted automation and content generation. However, the role retains a high degree of human-centric interaction, mentorship, and complex pedagogical judgment that requires physical presence and nuanced interpersonal engagement, preventing full automation.

title: Upholsterers and related workers
rationale: Upholstery is a highly manual, physical trade that requires tactile skill, spatial awareness, and the physical manipulation of materials. While AI may assist with administrative tasks like scheduling or digital pattern generation, the core work of fitting, sewing, and securing materials to physical frames remains largely immune to automation.

title: Valuers and loss assessors
rationale: This occupation involves significant information processing, data analysis, and report writing, which are domains where AI excels. While physical inspections remain necessary, AI-driven computer vision and automated valuation models are increasingly capable of assessing property conditions and estimating losses, leading to high potential for task automation and productivity gains.

title: Vehicle cleaners
rationale: Vehicle cleaning is a predominantly manual, physical occupation that requires direct interaction with physical objects in a non-digital environment. While automated car wash systems already exist, the core tasks of detailing and manual cleaning remain highly resistant to AI-driven automation.

title: Veterinarians
rationale: Veterinarians perform a significant amount of physical, hands-on work including surgery, physical examinations, and animal handling that cannot be automated. While AI will increasingly assist in diagnostic imaging, laboratory test analysis, and record-keeping, the core requirement for physical presence and complex manual intervention remains a strong barrier to full automation.

title: Veterinary technicians and assistants
rationale: Veterinary technicians and assistants perform a significant amount of physical, hands-on work, including restraining animals, cleaning facilities, and assisting in surgeries, which cannot be automated. While AI can assist with diagnostic image analysis and record-keeping, the core of the role requires real-time physical presence and interaction with live animals.

title: Visual artists
rationale: Visual artists face high exposure because generative AI models can now rapidly produce high-quality imagery, illustrations, and conceptual designs, which directly impacts the core creative output of many roles in this category. While physical mediums like sculpture and art restoration retain a barrier to automation, the digital aspects of illustration, storyboarding, and conceptual design are being fundamentally reshaped by AI tools.

title: Vocational education teachers
rationale: Vocational education teachers perform a significant amount of knowledge work, such as curriculum development, grading, and administrative reporting, which are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and augmentation. However, the role also requires substantial in-person instruction, physical demonstration of technical skills, and hands-on supervision of students, which creates a natural barrier to full AI replacement.

title: Waiters
rationale: The role of a waiter is fundamentally defined by physical presence, manual labor, and real-time interpersonal service in a dynamic environment. While AI and automation can assist with peripheral tasks like digital ordering systems or automated payment processing, the core service experience requires human interaction and physical dexterity that AI cannot replicate.

title: Water and firewood collectors
rationale: This occupation is entirely manual and physical, requiring direct interaction with the natural environment to collect and transport resources. AI technology has no capacity to automate or assist in these physical tasks, as they rely on human labor, mobility, and manual dexterity.

title: Weaving and knitting machine operators
rationale: This occupation is primarily focused on the physical operation, monitoring, and maintenance of industrial machinery in a manufacturing environment. While AI and advanced sensors can assist in predictive maintenance and quality control, the core tasks of threading machines, clearing jams, and physical machine upkeep remain largely manual and physical.

title: Web and multimedia developers
rationale: Web and multimedia development is a fundamentally digital profession centered on coding, design, and content creation, all of which are primary domains for AI advancement. Large language models and generative AI tools are already capable of writing significant portions of code, generating multimedia assets, and automating complex development workflows, leading to a major restructuring of the role.

title: Web technicians
rationale: Web technicians perform work that is almost entirely digital, involving server management, security monitoring, and website maintenance, all of which are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation. AI tools are already capable of automating system logs, security patching, performance analysis, and code-based website modifications, leading to a significant restructuring of the role.

title: Welders and flamecutters
rationale: Welding and flame cutting are highly manual, physical trades that require precise real-time manipulation of tools and materials in a physical environment. While AI-driven robotics can automate repetitive welding tasks in controlled factory settings, the core of the occupation remains deeply tied to physical skill, spatial awareness, and manual dexterity that AI cannot replicate.

title: Well drillers and borers and related workers
rationale: The work of well drillers and borers is primarily physical, involving the operation of heavy machinery and manual labor in unpredictable, outdoor environments. While AI may assist with monitoring gauges or optimizing drilling paths, the core tasks require physical presence, mechanical maintenance, and real-time human intervention that cannot be automated.

title: Window cleaners
rationale: Window cleaning is a physically demanding manual labor occupation that requires real-time interaction with the physical environment and specialized equipment. Because the core tasks involve manual dexterity and physical presence at heights, AI has virtually no capacity to automate or reshape the fundamental nature of this work.

title: Wood processing plant operators
rationale: This occupation is primarily focused on the physical operation and monitoring of heavy industrial machinery in a manufacturing environment. While AI and laser scanning are already used for optimizing cutting patterns, the core tasks require physical presence, manual oversight, and equipment maintenance that remain difficult to fully automate.

title: Wood treaters
rationale: This occupation is primarily focused on manual labor and the physical operation of industrial machinery in a manufacturing environment. While AI could potentially assist with monitoring gauges or optimizing kiln cycles, the core tasks require physical presence, material handling, and mechanical maintenance that are not easily automated by current AI systems.
